summary: Ukrainian FPV drones are expanding their operational range, targeting Russian logistics and rear areas up to 25-30 km from the front, including near Donetsk and on highways in the Pisky area. Russian forces report increasing difficulty countering Ukrainian drone dominance, with concerns over supply lines and rear-area security. Ukrainian operators using "Rubikon" centers conducted strikes across multiple directions, destroying 162 targets on March 10 and 114 on March 9, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs. Russian forces face challenges countering "Molnias" drones due to their passive signal reception, and Shahed drone attacks have decreased, possibly due to production issues, enhanced electronic warfare, or Starlink disconnection. A Russian soldier was hit by two simultaneous Ukrainian FPV drones while taking cover.
